description: All workflow files that commit/push declare permissions: contents: write
steps: Audited every .github/workflows/*.yml — sweep agent + manual review
Added permissions to update_district12.yml and triage.yml in earlier commit
All six workflows now have adequate permissions
Reason: today's failure was a codeload.github.com CDN outage, which hits SHA pins and major-version pins equally
SHA pinning trades transient-outage risk for losing security patches — wrong lever
Auto-rerun on transient detection (W1-3) is the right answer

description: Daily Alpha Dossier auto-recovers from transient action-download failures
steps: Implemented via the new notify_failure.yml workflow
Detects codeload outage signature in the failed job log
Auto-reruns the workflow ONCE if transient (run_attempt == 1)
Won't loop — subsequent failures of same run only file an issue

description: Workflow failures open or update a GitHub Issue with the run URL and first error
steps: Created .github/workflows/notify_failure.yml
Watches all six scheduled workflows via workflow_run trigger
Created 'pipeline-failure' label
Idempotent: one open issue per workflow, comments on existing

description: [ADDED] Fix watchlist_dive.yml deploy step (was missing environment + id)
steps: Surfaced by sweep audit — deploy-pages step had no environment block
Added environment: github-pages and id: deployment
Now matches daily_dossier.yml and deploy_pages.yml shape

description: TickerTrace client surfaces failures instead of swallowing them
steps: Added ::warning:: GHA annotation in fetch_institutional_data() so the failure shows as a yellow banner in the workflow UI
Annotation includes TICKERTRACE_API_BASE in the message — one-click debug
Added 'unavailable: true' flag in the empty return so downstream render can show 'TickerTrace unavailable' (consumer-side rendering optional follow-up)

description: Daily Alpha Dossier preflight step pings api.tickertrace.pro before running the pipeline
steps: Added 'Preflight — external API reachability' step in daily_dossier.yml after install deps
curl -fsS --max-time 10 https://api.tickertrace.pro/api/v1/stats
Result rendered into $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY (✅/❌ + fundsTracked count)
continue-on-error: true — pipeline still runs and degrades gracefully

description: Leveraged screener daily.html shows its own freshness banner
steps: Added <div id='freshness-banner' data-generated='{ISO}'> at top of .container
Inline JS reads data-generated, computes age
>25h (weekday) → red STALE banner with link to pipeline-failure issues
12–25h → yellow informational banner
Weekends excluded from stale check (cron is Mon–Fri)
Smoke-tested via _render_daily_html with fake data — 9306-byte page, banner + script present

description: Runbook doc — when a workflow fails, do these things in this order
steps: Wrote docs/runbooks/pipeline-failure.md
Covers Step 0 (auto-rerun check), failure-class table, fix-and-trigger flow, artifact map per workflow
Includes 'adding a new scheduled workflow' checklist
Appended items 7–10 to AGENTS.md Common Gotchas
perms: contents: write; TICKERTRACE_API_BASE host; codeload auto-rerun; freshness banner as alarm
Linked back to the runbook

description: Manual verification — yaml/python parse, render smoke-test, full live verification deferred to post-merge
steps: YAML-validated all 5 touched workflow files (notify_failure, daily_dossier, watchlist_dive, update_district12, triage)
AST-validated tickertrace.py, leveraged_daily_screener.py, config.py
End-to-end render test with fresh (today) and stale (2d ago) inputs — both produce 9306-byte page with banner div + JS
data-generated ISO timestamps confirmed correct in both cases

Note: live workflow_dispatch verification deferred to post-merge (notify_failure.yml only registers from default branch). After merge: manual dispatch district12, watchlist_dive, and confirm tomorrow's dossier preflight summary shows in run page.
